questions.question: What was Vahan Teryan's real full name?
questions.explanation: The text states his real name was Vahan Sukiasi Ter-Grigoryan.

questions.question: In what year was Vahan Teryan's first collection of works, "Twilight Dreams" (Մթնշաղի անուրջներ), published?
questions.explanation: His first collection, "Twilight Dreams," was published in 1908 in Tiflis.

questions.question: What was the title of Vahan Teryan's series of patriotic poems published in the "Mshak" newspaper in 1915?
questions.explanation: The patriotic poems series published in "Mshak" was titled "Land of Nairi."

questions.question: Which publishing house did Vahan Teryan found in 1912?
questions.explanation: In 1912, he founded the "Pantheon" publishing house.

questions.question: Vahan Teryan participated in the creation of literary collections compiled and edited by which two prominent figures between 1915-1916?
questions.explanation: He worked on books compiled by Valery Bryusov and Maxim Gorky.

questions.question: Which of the following works did Vahan Teryan translate from Armenian to Russian?
questions.explanation: He translated Gabriel Sundukyan's "Pepo," Raffi's "Sparks" (first volume), and Shirvanzade's "Evil Spirit."

questions.question: What medical condition did Vahan Teryan begin to show signs of in 1916?
questions.explanation: In 1916, signs of tuberculosis began to appear in Vahan.

questions.question: Which major political event prompted Vahan Teryan to abandon his treatment in the Caucasus and travel to Petersburg?
options: The signing of the Brest Peace Treaty
questions.explanation: The February Revolution prompted him to leave his treatment.

questions.question: From the first days of Soviet power, Vahan Teryan became a close associate of which prominent political figure?
questions.explanation: He became a close associate of Stalin from the first days of Soviet power.

questions.question: In 1919, Vahan Teryan was assigned to go to Turkestan, but due to severe illness, he remained and eventually died in which city?
questions.explanation: He was forced to stay in Orenburg due to severe illness, where he passed away on January 7, 1920.
